issues.official_page: AXIS Foils official FB page (axisfoils) inaccessible - URL consistently redirecting to Facebook Marketplace listings. Possible causes: page privacy settings, URL slug conflict, or account restrictions. All direct URL formats tried: /axisfoils, /axisfoils/, profile.php?id=100063600613802, /AXIS-Foils-109895724736427 — all returned either Marketplace or 'content not available'.

posts_last_12h.post_text:
Hybrid Carbon foil boards
Was wondering anyone in the know if the green sea foam color Axis hybrid carbon foil boards were designed by Frank that did the darts or another shaper? Anyone using them for parawing
Happy birthday to this Hawaiian hammah Jason foil fever … stoked to get to ride with him all week here in Southern California. New integrated mast so good he won't let anyone else demo it. 😄

comments.text: That mast is a game changer!
Happy Birthday Jason, enjoy that California weed....

posts_12h_to_24h.post_text:
Hi all. Has anyone got experience using PNGv2 1200 for wake or wing foiling?
I'd like to get one for dock starts but would like to know what it's like for other winging and wake foiling. Thanks!
I'm a beginner at dock starts. intermediate wing foiler and tow foiler. I use art pro v2 999 and HPS1050. I'm 77kg

comments.text: I have used it for both. Wing in sub 10 knot wind. It's very slow and turns have to be deliberate and slow. Wake is fine. You can 15 wakes back but same thing. It likes to go straight and is super hard to turn.

Wide wings are a PITA to control on boat wakes, too wide to control well. Even the PNG 1150 was hard to ride on a boat wake. The couple times I've tried wider wings it wasn't worth it. Now to pull up in the flats and drop the handle to just pump is…
comments.note: text truncated - See more button not expanded

comments.text: What fuse? Water looks nice and warm
That is up in the lagoons up in the highlands, Andes. Water definitely is not warm.lol

posts_12h_to_24h.post_text: New name, same data. Enjoy! www.foildata.app

posts_12h_to_24h.post_text:
Axis/Amos Nitro tip - I recently bought an Amos Nitro for prone and noticed straight away it didn't feel right. Mast pushed forward was way too much lift (Surge890) and twitchy as hell. Moving the mast back I noticed the Nitro tapers off (rocker) which causes the board to run nose high when on foil. This also affects takeoffs, pumping and makes foot position unforgiving.
The Answer: 1deg Mast shim! Now takeoffs are smooth and predictable, foot position is forgiving, most im… [see more]

comments.text: Yep in the early days of foiling, boards still had some tail rocker, it sucked!

I had axis art v2 with JS Eagle 2 a while back, always wanted to nosedive, even when fully forward in the tracks. Looked like a flat board rocker. Improved with a mast shim but still frustrating. I'm on amos raptor and surge, no shim, seems a much bett…

posts_12h_to_24h.post_text:
Fatty Mast and foil drive!
Hi legends,
Any power athletes at 100kg or about have experience with the fatty mast + fuse with a foil drive, max or fusion they could share please?
Thanks Mick.

comments.text: Not sure I am a power athlete but I am around that weight and have extensive experience with the AXIS Foils Fatty mast and fuse! I have used it with the Fusion, Max and Slim..... not sure the unit you bolt it too makes any difference. The Universal pod… [see more]
Deon Aumaier has a fatty and Foildrives with it I believe. Using 1750

Making the most of the wind season here in WA. Now looking for some storm winds In the next few months! Loving wing foiling in the swan river!
Catching some air on the Swan River with my trusty Axis and Duotone gear! #Wingfoiling #swanriver #australia #axis #duotone
